Just now

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Multi-tenancy is one of the first architectural decisions that becomes expensive to change later.
If every customer shares the same application, you need a reliable way to resolve the tenant, enforce isolation, query tenant-scoped data, and prevent one customer from accidentally seeing another customer’s records.
In this article, we will build a production-style multi-tenant API in ASP.NET Core using tenant resolution middleware, strongly typed tenant context, EF Core global query filters, write-side guards, and operational tradeoffs.
The Architecture
We will use a shared database and shared schema model. Every tenant-owned table has a TenantId column. The API resolves the tenant from the authenticated user or request host, stores it in a scoped context, and EF Core automatically filters tenant-owned queries.
This is the most common starting point for SaaS applications because it is simple to operate and cost-efficient.
The Tenant Context
The current tenant should be explicit. Avoid passing raw strings across the codebase.
public sealed record TenantContext(
string TenantId,
string Name,
string Plan);
